concept.name: Causal Quotient Feature (intervention-equivalence class)
concept.definition: Fix layer L and a behavior metric m (e.g. logit-diff on an eval set). Declare two perturbations equivalent, d1 ~ d2, iff max over eval contexts |m(h+d1) - m(h+d2)| <= epsilon. The representation of a concept is the equivalence class of the minimal-norm perturbation achieving a target behavioral shift, reported as (effective causal rank k = number of non-negligible singular values of the local perturbation-to-effect Jacobian, canonical representative = its top singular vector). 'Which direction IS the representation' becomes a category error: probe/steer/SAE vectors are different coset representatives of one quotient class.
concept.prediction: Computable: for concepts where probe direction, difference-of-means steering vector, and matched SAE decoder row have pairwise cosine < 0.6 (the field's current embarrassment), >= 90% of the squared norm of their pairwise DIFFERENCE vectors lies in the causal null space (bottom singular subspace of the perturbation-effect Jacobian), and projecting all three onto the top-k causal subspace raises pairwise cosines to >= 0.9. FALSIFIED if the difference vectors carry substantial causal effect (i.e., the three objects genuinely do different things rather than being null-space-shifted copies).
concept.reduces_to: Causal abstraction / DAS (Geiger et al.) and activation patching — risk it is causal mediation analysis wearing a quotient-space costume.

test.design: Preregistered BEFORE running (/home/friemann/workspace/repos-demidovz/maevtica/research_cycle/experiments/cqf_nullspace/PREREG.md; code exp.py, numbers result.json). gpt2-small L6 resid_post, 3 concepts (sentiment adj, past/present tense, animal/vehicle noun; 24+24 sentences each, 18/6 train/test). Three objects per concept: diff-of-means m, logistic probe direction p, matched OpenAI-TopK-32k SAE decoder row s (latent = max train activation difference); unit-normalized, sign-aligned to m. Qualifying pairs = within-concept pairs with cos < 0.6 (the prediction's premise). Causal Jacobian decoupled from the concepts: finite-difference linearized map (all 768 basis directions, eps = 0.02 x resid norm, batched) from L6 last-position resid to last-token logits on 4 NEUTRAL prompts; Gram G = sum J^T J; top-k causal subspace = eigvecs covering 90% of spectrum (k90), null space = rest. Per-pair metrics: nf = null-space fraction of the difference vector's squared norm; cos_topk = cosine after projecting both objects onto the top-k subspace; rel_ce = sqrt(d^T G d)/max object causal effect. DECISION RULE (fixed in advance): BROKEN_MEASUREMENT if oracles fail; SUPPORTED iff median nf >= 0.90 AND median cos_topk >= 0.90 AND median rel_ce < 0.5; REFUTED iff median cos_topk < 0.60 OR median rel_ce >= 0.5; INCONCLUSIVE otherwise or if < 3 qualifying pairs. Calibration guard against a vacuous null-space clause: random-direction nf must be reported (was 0.55, so the clause was discriminative, not trivially satisfiable).
test.numbers: k90 = 342 of 768 (null space = 426 dims). Premise reproduced: 4 qualifying pairs (m-s and p-s for sentiment cos~0.30 and animal/vehicle cos~0.42; tense pairs at 0.62 did not qualify; all m-p pairs cos>0.998 — probe and diff-of-means are near-identical here, the divergent object is the SAE row). Results vs prediction: median nf = 0.52 (predicted >= 0.90) — the difference vectors are NOT null-space-concentrated, they sit at chance-level null fraction (random-direction median nf = 0.55). Median cos_topk = 0.43 (predicted >= 0.90; raw cosines 0.30-0.43 rose only to 0.38-0.48 after top-k causal projection). Median rel_ce = 0.93 — the difference direction carries ~93% as much linearized causal effect as the objects themselves. This is exactly the preregistered falsification condition: the three objects genuinely do different things; they are not null-space-shifted copies. REFUTED on both refutation triggers (cos_topk < 0.60 AND rel_ce >= 0.5). Limits: one model (gpt2-small), one layer, linearized effect map at 4 neutral prompts, last-token logits, 3 concepts, one SAE, one seed — refuted-in-this-regime, though the margin is not close on any metric.
test.sanity: All oracles PASSED, so the refutation is not a broken-measurement artifact. O1 linearity of the finite-difference Jacobian: effects at eps vs 2*eps, median cos 0.9998 and norm ratio 2.000 (required >= 0.98 and 1.7-2.3). O2 the "causal null space" is behaviorally null at REAL steering scale (alpha = 0.5 x resid norm, not the linearization eps): ||delta logits|| along top-1 eigvec = 597 vs bottom-1 eigvec = 21.6, ratio 27.7 (required >= 5) — the top/bottom split measures genuine causal asymmetry. O3 concept gate: probe held-out accuracy 1.0 on all 3 concepts; matched SAE latents had large activation differences (18.6-24.1). Calibration guard: random-direction null fraction 0.55 < 0.9, so the >= 90%-in-null-space clause was falsifiable rather than vacuous at this k90.

tested.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)

concept.name: Process-Pullback Geometry (shape law: the data process decides, not the model)
concept.definition: The representation of a concept is the affine pullback into activation space of the belief-state coordinates of the minimal generative model (epsilon-machine / mixed-state presentation) of the relevant sublanguage of the training distribution: fit an affine map from activations to belief-simplex coordinates; the concept's geometry (line, circle, fractal, simplex face) is DEFINED as the image of the belief set under the inverse map. This converts the LRH from an hypothesis about models into a computable function of the data: linear features are exactly the concepts whose minimal process has a 1-simplex belief set.
concept.prediction: Pre-registered shape forecasting: pick >= 5 structured domains where the mixed-state geometry is computable before touching the network (e.g. base-12 clock arithmetic -> circle; a 3-state Mess3-style HMM -> fractal; a binary flag process -> line). Predict, in advance, the intrinsic dimension and topology (Betti numbers via persistent homology) of the activation manifold a transformer trained on that domain will exhibit for the concept. Match rate must beat chance with p < 0.01. FALSIFIED by a single clean case of a model reaching the same next-token loss while representing a provably-circular process on a line (or vice versa) — that would show model-internal factors, not data geometry, set the shape.
concept.reduces_to: Shai et al. belief-state fractal geometry itself — the only delta is promoting it from post-hoc explanation of one experiment to a forward-predictive definition of 'the representation'; if that promotion fails, it renamed nothing.

test.design: Preregistered (BEFORE any model training) in /tmp/claude-1000/-home-friemann-workspace-maestratica/b9bd7464-4c05-40a5-ba2b-b36c0ae2836c/scratchpad/PREREG_shape_forecast.md; code in shape_forecast.py (same dir). 5 domains with process geometry computable analytically: D1 clock12 -> circle (Betti b0=1,b1=1), D2 clock8 -> circle (1,1), D3 absorbing flag -> 2 clusters (2,0), D4 reflecting bounded counter -> line (1,0), D5 two independent flags -> 4 clusters (4,0). Each trained from scratch on a 2-layer d=64 GPT (seed 0, 3000 steps, one preregistered 8000-step extension); state centroids from final-block residuals, positions 20-62; geometry classifier: PCA-whiten axes >5% top variance, b0 = single-linkage components at 0.55*diam, b1 = Rips H1 bars with persistence >0.25*diam (n>=6 required). LOCKED DECISION RULE: loss gate dCE<0.05 nats vs analytic optimum; SUPPORTED iff k>=4 learned domains and all k match (binomial null 1/4 per domain: p=(1/4)^k<=0.0039<0.01); REFUTED iff any learned clock domain shows b1=0 at matched loss (the preregistered falsifier: provably-circular process on a line) or matches <=1; INCONCLUSIVE otherwise. Chance model: 4 distinct predicted signatures, uniform guess.
test.numbers: Calibration/control phase RAN and passed fully; the decisive 5-model training phase was launched (background task by78d7s4k, output at .../scratchpad/run_output.txt) but had not finished within the time box, so NO model-geometry numbers exist yet — verdict on the concept is honestly inconclusive, not claimed. Control numbers: oracle 5/5 ground-truth configs classified to predicted signature (clock12 H1 persistence/diam=0.392, clock8=0.315, both >T1=0.25; flag/counter/twoflag exact); stress shapes 5/5 (noisy ellipse-aspect-4 n=12 H1r=0.543, n=8 0.481, line9/square4/2blobs correct); negative control false-circle rate 0/200 at n=8 and 0/200 at n=12 (threshold <5%). To finish: rerun `~/.local/state/mst/crc-venv311/bin/python shape_forecast.py run` (~20-40 CPU-min); thresholds are locked, so whatever it prints is the verdict.
test.sanity: Oracle/positive control worked AND earned its keep: v1 classifier was BROKEN and only the oracle caught it — (a) a 4-point square generically produces a spurious Rips H1 bar (pers/diam=0.293, overlapping a true 12-point circle at 0.266, not threshold-separable -> fixed by requiring n>=6 for H1 evidence), (b) clock emission curve at softness 1.2 is a 3-fold-symmetric rounded triangle whose 8-point sampling honestly reads (2,0) -> domain softened to 0.7 pre-model, and (c) whitened clock8 MST bottleneck 0.484*diam vs cluster separations 0.707 -> T0 set 0.55 between them. All fixes used control data only, locked before any model activation existed; final battery: oracle 5/5, stress 5/5, Gaussian FPR 0/400. This is exactly the .norm-bug-class guard doing its job — twice.
